info.title: NanoGPT API
info.description: API documentation for the NanoGPT language, image, video, speech-to-text, and text-to-speech generation services

post.description: Creates a chat completion for the provided messages. The NanoGPT Advisor extension is available for non-streaming, platform-billed pay-as-you-go API-key requests that do not use client tools, structured output, inline moderation, BYOK, accountless payment, memory, or server-side content enhancements.

parameters.name: X-Provider
parameters.description: Optional explicit provider override for supported open-source models (case-insensitive). Explicit provider selection is billed pay-as-you-go at the selected provider's price, including provider-selection markup; for subscription users it bypasses subscription coverage for that request.

parameters.name: X-Billing-Mode
parameters.description: Optional billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic (e.g., paygo). Header name is case-insensitive.

parameters.name: x-x402
parameters.description: Set to true on unauthenticated accountless x402 quote requests. Without this header, unauthenticated requests return 401 missing_api_key.

402.description: Payment Required - authenticated insufficient balance or opted-in accountless x402 quote for supported non-streaming requests

post.description: Creates a completion for the provided prompt. This endpoint is available on a best-effort basis for legacy compatibility, and performance may be less consistent than /v1/chat/completions because not all upstream providers support the legacy completions API.

parameters.name: X-Provider
parameters.description: Optional explicit provider override for supported open-source models (case-insensitive). Explicit provider selection is billed pay-as-you-go at the selected provider's price, including provider-selection markup; for subscription users it bypasses subscription coverage for that request.

parameters.name: X-Billing-Mode
parameters.description: Optional billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic (e.g., paygo). Header name is case-insensitive.

post.description: Legacy endpoint for chat interactions with the GPT model

schema.description: Text response followed by metadata in <NanoGPT> tags

get.description: List available models with optional detailed information including pricing

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: Returns detailed model information including pricing

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Reorders the returned model list without adding or hiding models. `favorites` orders by the authenticated account's recent model usage and requires a valid API key; without one it falls back to normal ordering. `mostused` orders by global completed model usage and does not require authentication. Unused or unranked models remain after ranked models in normal catalog order. Invalid values are ignored.

get.description: Retrieve aggregate spend, request, and token usage for the authenticated API key, grouped by UTC day, model, or both.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: UTC start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Must be provided together with `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: UTC end date in YYYY-MM-DD format, inclusive. Must be on or after `from` and cannot be in the future.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Controls which aggregate arrays are returned. `model,day` is accepted as an alias for `day,model`.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Usage scope. `current_key` and `api_key` both return usage for the authenticated API key.

parameters.name: api_key_id
parameters.description: Optional current API key ID. Requests for another API key are rejected.

200.description: Aggregate usage for the authenticated API key

400.description: Invalid date range, unsupported parameter, invalid grouping, or request for a different API key

401.description: Missing or invalid API key

503.description: Usage API temporarily unavailable or requested rollup data is not ready yet

get.description: Explore text model records. Supports filtering to models whose model-level distillation policy is allowed.

parameters.name: distillation
parameters.description: When set to `allowed`, returns only text models whose model-level distillation policy is allowed. Without this filter, text model responses report `meta.distillation` as `all`.

get.description: Search explore records. The distillation filter applies only when searching text models.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Explore result type. Use `text` with `distillation=allowed` to search distillation-allowed text models.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query.

parameters.name: distillation
parameters.description: When set to `allowed` with `type=text`, returns only text models whose model-level distillation policy is allowed.

200.description: Provider availability and pricing for the model

post.description: Generate videos using supported text-to-video, image-to-video, and video-to-video models. The response includes a runId and pending status; poll the status endpoint for completion. See the docs for the current model list and required inputs.

requestBody.description: Parameters for video generation across different models and providers

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters or safety filter triggered

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

parameters.description: Model identifier used in the generation request (e.g., 'veo2-video')

200.description: Current status for the requested video generation

400.description: Bad Request - Missing or invalid parameters

post.description: Creates an image generation for the provided prompt (OpenAI-compatible). For unauthenticated accountless x402 quote requests, include x-x402: true.

parameters.name: x-x402
parameters.description: Set to true on unauthenticated accountless x402 quote requests. Without this header, unauthenticated requests return 401 missing_api_key.

200.description: Image generation response. Each data[i] contains either { url } or { b64_json }. When requesting response_format: "url", the API may fall back to returning { b64_json } if URL generation (upload/presign) fails.

402.description: Payment Required - authenticated insufficient balance or opted-in accountless x402 quote

post.description: Edit images with JSON inputs. Multipart uploads require normal authentication before conversion; accountless x402 applies to JSON edit requests only when the unauthenticated quote request includes x-x402: true.

post.description: Transcribe audio (and supported video formats) into text using speech recognition models. Supports multiple languages, diarization (model-dependent), and various formats. Most models return synchronous results; some models (for example Elevenlabs-STT and voice cloning workflows) return asynchronous job IDs.

requestBody.description: Audio transcription parameters. Use multipart/form-data for file uploads or application/json for URL-based requests.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters or file format

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

413.description: Payload Too Large - File exceeds size limit

404.description: Not Found - Transcription job not found

post.description: Create a response with the OpenAI-compatible Responses API. The NanoGPT Advisor extension is available for non-streaming, foreground, platform-billed pay-as-you-go API-key requests that do not use client tools, structured output, inline moderation, BYOK, accountless payment, memory, or server-side content enhancements.

parameters.name: X-Provider
parameters.description: Optional explicit provider override for supported open-source models (case-insensitive). Explicit provider selection is billed pay-as-you-go at the selected provider's price, including provider-selection markup; for subscription users it bypasses subscription coverage for that request.

parameters.name: X-Billing-Mode
parameters.description: Optional billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic (e.g., paygo). Header name is case-insensitive.

parameters.name: X-Team-Id
parameters.description: Optional team context override for API-key requests. If provided, it must reference a team the caller belongs to.

parameters.name: x-x402
parameters.description: Set to true on unauthenticated accountless x402 quote requests. Without this header, unauthenticated requests return 401 missing_api_key.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

402.description: Payment Required - authenticated insufficient balance or opted-in accountless x402 quote for supported non-streaming requests

parameters.description: Response ID

model.description: The model to use for completion. The model value may include supported model suffixes, including web search (':online', ':online/<provider>'), memory (':memory', ':memory-<days>'), reasoning visibility (':reasoning-exclude'), thinking variants where listed by the model catalog (':thinking'), and provider routing preferences for eligible models (':fast', ':cheap', ':caching', etc.).

billing_mode.description: Billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic. Accepted values (case-insensitive): paygo, pay-as-you-go, pay_as_you_go, paid, payg.

provider.description: Optional provider override or structured provider routing controls for provider-selection-capable models. A string explicitly selects one provider. An object can set soft order, hard pins, exclusions, sort preference, price caps, fallback behavior, and parameter-capability requirements.

oneOf.description: Provider ID or accepted provider display name/alias.

caching.description: When true, route to an available provider that is marked as prompt/input-caching capable for the requested provider-selection model. If no usable cache-capable provider exists, the request fails instead of falling back to a non-cache-capable provider. This is provider capability routing only; it does not add cache_control markers, configure cache TTLs, or force a cache write. The model suffixes ':caching', ':cache', and ':cached' request the same behavior.

stickyprovider.description: Top-level routing control for caching: true. When caching is true, defaults to true and prefers the previously recorded provider for later matching requests from the same API key or session when still usable. Set false to require a cache-capable provider without sticky routing.

stickyProvider.description: CamelCase alias for top-level stickyprovider. Distinct from prompt_caching.stickyProvider, which controls explicit prompt-cache failover behavior.

messages.description: Array of message objects with role and content

role.description: The role of the message author

content.description: Message content as a simple string or an array of multimodal parts

service_tier.description: Optional service tier: "auto", "default", "flex", or "priority". Use "flex" for lower-cost variable-capacity processing or "priority" for higher-cost priority processing where supported by the routed model/provider.

temperature.description: Classic randomness control. Accepts any decimal between 0-2. If omitted, NanoGPT does not force a value and the routed provider/model default applies

max_tokens.description: Upper bound on generated tokens. If omitted, NanoGPT does not enforce an explicit default and the routed provider/model default applies

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling. When set below 1.0, trims candidate tokens to the smallest set whose cumulative probability exceeds top_p. Works well as an alternative to tweaking temperature

frequency_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens proportionally to how often they appeared previously. Negative values encourage repetition; positive values discourage it

presence_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on whether they appeared at all. Good for keeping the model on topic without outright banning words

repetition_penalty.description: Provider-agnostic repetition modifier (distinct from OpenAI penalties). Values >1 discourage repetition

top_k.description: Caps sampling to the top-k highest probability tokens per step

top_a.description: Combines top-p and temperature behavior; leave unset unless a model description explicitly calls for it

min_p.description: Ensures each candidate token probability exceeds a floor (0-1). Helpful for stopping models from collapsing into low-entropy loops

tfs.description: Tail free sampling. Values between 0-1 let you shave the long tail of the distribution; 1.0 disables the feature

eta_cutoff.description: Cut probabilities as soon as they fall below the specified tail threshold

epsilon_cutoff.description: Cut probabilities as soon as they fall below the specified tail threshold

typical_p.description: Typical sampling (aka entropy-based nucleus). Works like top_p but preserves tokens whose surprise matches the expected entropy

mirostat_mode.description: Enables Mirostat sampling for models that support it. Set to 1 or 2 to activate

mirostat_tau.description: Mirostat target entropy parameter. Used when mirostat_mode is enabled

mirostat_eta.description: Mirostat learning rate parameter. Used when mirostat_mode is enabled

min_tokens.description: For providers that support it, enforces a minimum completion length before stop conditions fire

stop.description: Stop sequences. Accepts string or array of strings. Values are passed directly to upstream providers

stop_token_ids.description: Numeric array that lets callers stop generation on specific token IDs. Not supported by many providers

include_stop_str_in_output.description: When true, keeps the stop sequence in the final text. Not supported by many providers

ignore_eos.description: Allows completions to continue even if the model predicts EOS internally. Useful for long creative writing runs

no_repeat_ngram_size.description: Extension that forbids repeating n-grams of the given size. Not supported by many providers

custom_token_bans.description: List of token IDs to fully block

logit_bias.description: Object mapping token IDs to additive logits. Works just like OpenAI's version

logprobs.description: When true or a number, forwards the request to providers that support returning token-level log probabilities

prompt_logprobs.description: Requests logprobs on the prompt itself when the upstream API allows it

seed.description: Optional integer forwarded to providers that support seeded sampling. This may improve reproducibility but does not guarantee identical output. Results can change if NanoGPT selects a different route, uses a fallback, or the provider changes its backend.

prompt_caching.description: Helper to tag leading messages for explicit prompt-caching control (primarily Claude flows). Providers with implicit caching support (including OpenAI, Gemini, and many open-source provider routes) do not require this helper. NanoGPT injects cache_control blocks on each message up to the specified index before forwarding upstream. If cut_after_message_index is omitted, NanoGPT selects a cache boundary automatically.

enabled.description: Whether to enable prompt caching on this request

cut_after_message_index.description: Zero-based index of the last message that should be cached. All messages up to and including this index receive the same cache_control block.

stickyProvider.description: When true, avoids failover to preserve prompt cache consistency. If a fallback would be required, the request can return 503 instead.

reasoning_effort.description: Controls reasoning depth and acts as an explicit reasoning-mode signal. Any value other than "none" requests reasoning/thinking behavior. Use "none" to explicitly disable reasoning.

reasoning.description: Reasoning configuration. `exclude` controls output visibility (hides reasoning fields/blocks) and does not inherently disable reasoning compute. `effort` controls depth, and `delta_field` switches to legacy `reasoning_content` fields.

exclude.description: When true, omits reasoning fields from the response

effort.description: Alias for reasoning_effort. If both are provided, top-level reasoning_effort is authoritative for Chat Completions request shaping.

delta_field.description: When set to "reasoning_content", uses legacy reasoning_content fields instead of reasoning

reasoning_content_compat.description: Shorthand to force legacy reasoning_content fields in the response

AdvisorConfig.description: Allows the executor model to consult one different advisor model. Auto mode lets the executor decide whether to consult; required mode forces one consultation request. Advisor is non-streaming and available only for platform-billed pay-as-you-go API-key requests. Subscriptions, BYOK, accountless x402, Private Mode, inline moderation, client tools, structured outputs, memory, and server-side content enhancements are rejected before orchestration. Each completed executor, advisor, and continuation phase is billed separately.

model.description: Explicit advisor model ID. Automatic model selection and the executor's resolved model are not allowed.

mode.description: Auto lets the executor decide whether to consult. Required forces exactly one internal consultation request before the final answer.

max_completion_tokens.description: Optional advisor output-token limit. When omitted, NanoGPT does not impose an Advisor-specific output cap and the selected model/provider defaults apply.

AdvisorResponseMetadata.description: Advisor orchestration outcome. Top-level usage and pricing aggregate billable phases that returned usage, including an HTTP-success advisor phase whose output was unusable; the optional per-phase objects provide reconciliation detail.

requested.description: Whether the executor emitted the internal consultation request.

consulted.description: Whether an advisor child call was attempted.

successful.description: Whether the advisor returned usable advice.

error.description: Present when consultation failed but the executor could continue.

url.description: HTTPS URL or base64 data URL for the image

video_url.description: HTTPS URL or data URL for the video

cache_control.description: Prompt caching control applied to this content block for explicit caching flows (primarily Claude).

type.description: Cache type. Claude currently exposes the 'ephemeral' tier for 5m/1h TTLs.

ttl.description: Optional TTL override for this block.

created.description: Unix timestamp of when the completion was created

reasoning.description: Optional reasoning output (when supported and not excluded)

reasoning_content.description: Legacy alias of reasoning (only present when legacy compatibility is enabled)

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt

prompt_tokens_details.description: Optional details about prompt token accounting

cached_tokens.description: Cached input tokens (when prompt caching is used)

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Input tokens written to cache on this request (when prompt caching is used)

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Input tokens read from cache on this request (when prompt caching is used)

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the completion

completion_tokens_details.description: Optional breakdown of completion token accounting

reasoning_tokens.description: Reasoning tokens within the completion (when reported)
Reasoning tokens within the completion (when reported)

service_tier.description: Service tier used (echoed when provided on the request)

advisor.description: Advisor orchestration status and per-phase usage. Present on responses to requests that use the Advisor extension.

model.description: The model to use for completion. The model value may include supported model suffixes, including web search (':online', ':online/<provider>'), memory (':memory', ':memory-<days>'), reasoning visibility (':reasoning-exclude'), thinking variants where listed by the model catalog (':thinking'), and provider routing preferences for eligible models (':fast', ':cheap', ':caching', etc.).

billing_mode.description: Billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic. Accepted values (case-insensitive): paygo, pay-as-you-go, pay_as_you_go, paid, payg.

provider.description: Optional provider override or structured provider routing controls for provider-selection-capable models. A string explicitly selects one provider. An object can set soft order, hard pins, exclusions, sort preference, price caps, fallback behavior, and parameter-capability requirements.

oneOf.description: Provider ID or accepted provider display name/alias.

max_tokens.description: Upper bound on generated tokens. If omitted, NanoGPT does not enforce an explicit default and the routed provider/model default applies

temperature.description: Classic randomness control. Accepts any decimal between 0-2. If omitted, NanoGPT does not force a value and the routed provider/model default applies

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling. When set below 1.0, trims candidate tokens to the smallest set whose cumulative probability exceeds top_p. Works well as an alternative to tweaking temperature

stop.description: Stop sequences. Accepts string or array of strings. Values are passed directly to upstream providers

frequency_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens proportionally to how often they appeared previously. Negative values encourage repetition; positive values discourage it

presence_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on whether they appeared at all. Good for keeping the model on topic without outright banning words

repetition_penalty.description: Provider-agnostic repetition modifier (distinct from OpenAI penalties). Values >1 discourage repetition

top_k.description: Caps sampling to the top-k highest probability tokens per step

top_a.description: Combines top-p and temperature behavior; leave unset unless a model description explicitly calls for it

min_p.description: Ensures each candidate token probability exceeds a floor (0-1). Helpful for stopping models from collapsing into low-entropy loops

tfs.description: Tail free sampling. Values between 0-1 let you shave the long tail of the distribution; 1.0 disables the feature

eta_cutoff.description: Cut probabilities as soon as they fall below the specified tail threshold

epsilon_cutoff.description: Cut probabilities as soon as they fall below the specified tail threshold

typical_p.description: Typical sampling (aka entropy-based nucleus). Works like top_p but preserves tokens whose surprise matches the expected entropy

mirostat_mode.description: Enables Mirostat sampling for models that support it. Set to 1 or 2 to activate

mirostat_tau.description: Mirostat target entropy parameter. Used when mirostat_mode is enabled

mirostat_eta.description: Mirostat learning rate parameter. Used when mirostat_mode is enabled

min_tokens.description: For providers that support it, enforces a minimum completion length before stop conditions fire

stop_token_ids.description: Numeric array that lets callers stop generation on specific token IDs. Not supported by many providers

include_stop_str_in_output.description: When true, keeps the stop sequence in the final text. Not supported by many providers

ignore_eos.description: Allows completions to continue even if the model predicts EOS internally. Useful for long creative writing runs

no_repeat_ngram_size.description: Extension that forbids repeating n-grams of the given size. Not supported by many providers

custom_token_bans.description: List of token IDs to fully block

logit_bias.description: Object mapping token IDs to additive logits. Works just like OpenAI's version

logprobs.description: When true or a number, forwards the request to providers that support returning token-level log probabilities

prompt_logprobs.description: Requests logprobs on the prompt itself when the upstream API allows it

seed.description: Optional integer forwarded to providers that support seeded sampling. This may improve reproducibility but does not guarantee identical output. Results can change if NanoGPT selects a different route, uses a fallback, or the provider changes its backend.

created.description: Unix timestamp of when the completion was created

model.description: Model used for completion

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the completion

prompt.description: The text description of the image to generate
prompt.example: RAW photo, a portrait photo of a latina woman in casual clothes, natural skin, 8k uhd, high quality, film grain, Fujifilm XT3

model.description: The model to use for generation

negative_prompt.description: Things to avoid in the generated image

imageDataUrl.description: Base64-encoded image data URL for img2img generation (format: data:image/[type];base64,[data])

kontext_max_mode.description: When using flux-kontext model, enables maximum context mode for better image understanding

message.example: from and to must use YYYY-MM-DD UTC dates.

model.description: Public model label for model-grouped buckets.

requests.description: Number of billable usage requests in the aggregate bucket.

costUsd.description: Gross USD usage cost before refunds.

refundedUsd.description: USD amount refunded in the aggregate bucket.

netCostUsd.description: max(0, costUsd - refundedUsd) for the aggregate bucket.

inputTokens.description: Input tokens counted for the bucket.

outputTokens.description: Output tokens counted for the bucket.

reasoningTokens.description: Reasoning tokens counted separately when available.

totalTokens.description: inputTokens + outputTokens. Reasoning tokens are reported separately and are not added to totalTokens.

rollupDays.description: Days served from precomputed daily rollups.

liveDays.description: Days served from live aggregation, usually today or days not rolled up yet.

missingRollupDays.description: Closed UTC days that were not available in the rollup table and had to be served live.

id.description: Numeric ID of the authenticated API key.

asOf.description: Timestamp when the aggregate response was generated. Cached responses can be up to 60 seconds old.

byDayModel.description: Returned when grouped by both day and model.

prompt.description: The text prompt to send to GPT (optional)
prompt.example: Please explain the concept of artificial intelligence.

model.description: The model to use for generation. The legacy endpoint supports the same model suffix parser for web search and provider routing, including ':caching', ':cache', and ':cached', as Chat Completions.

provider.description: Optional provider override or structured provider routing controls for provider-selection-capable models. A string explicitly selects one provider. An object can set soft order, hard pins, exclusions, sort preference, price caps, fallback behavior, and parameter-capability requirements.

oneOf.description: Provider ID or accepted provider display name/alias.

messages.description: Array of previous message objects for context (optional)

role.description: The role of the message author

object.description: Type of object, always 'list' for the models response

created.description: Unix timestamp when the model was created

description.example: OpenAI's affordable and intelligent small model for fast, lightweight tasks

context_length.description: Maximum input tokens (detailed mode only, null if not available)

prompt.description: Cost per million input tokens in USD

completion.description: Cost per million output tokens in USD

data.description: Text model records returned by explore.

distillation.description: Distillation filter state. `allowed` when the filter is active, otherwise `all` for text model responses.

DistillationPolicy.description: API-only policy metadata indicating whether model or provider-route outputs may be used for model training, fine-tuning, distillation, or similar model-improvement workflows under NanoGPT's recorded model-license and provider-terms rules.

status.description: Whether distillation is allowed under the current recorded rule set.

basis.description: Rule basis used to derive the policy status.

sourceUrl.description: License or provider terms URL used as the policy source, when recorded.

note.description: Short explanation and caveats for the policy signal.
note.example: Short explanation of the policy signal.

nanoDepositAddress.description: Nano deposit address for the account

image_urls.description: Primary input field. One URL or an array of URLs (only the first 10 are processed).

imageUrls.description: Alias for image_urls. One URL or an array of URLs (only the first 10 are processed).

imageUrl.description: Alias for a single image URL

imageDataUrl.description: Base64 data URL for a single image (format: data:image/[type];base64,[data])

imageDataUrls.description: Base64 data URLs for multiple images (only the first 10 are processed)

model.description: Only supported value is nsfw-classifier
The classifier model used

requestId.description: Request ID (if available; helpful for support)

cost.description: Final charged amount (after discounts and currency conversion)

truncated.description: True if more than 10 images were provided

has_nsfw_concepts.description: Per-image NSFW flags in input order

is_nsfw.description: True if any image was flagged

model.description: The video model to use for generation. See the docs for the current model list and required inputs.

prompt.description: Text prompt describing the video to generate
prompt.example: A serene lake at sunset with gentle ripples on the water

script.description: Fully-written script for LongStories models (takes precedence over prompt)

shortRequestEnhancer.description: Smart Enhancement: if true, automatically choose better framework and add Director Notes if necessary

targetLengthInWords.description: Target length in words for LongStories models (legacy parameter)

targetLengthInSeconds.description: Target length in seconds (alternative to words)

directorNotes.description: Prompt for the image generation engine (LongStories). Example: 'Warm lighting' or 'Make the first image very impactful'
directorNotes.example: Warm, cozy lighting with focus on people interacting

style.description: Sets the tone & voice of the generated script

targetLengthInSeconds.description: Approx. time length of the script in seconds. The actual length may vary slightly.

model.description: Which image model to use for generating images

loraConfig.description: Style configuration (only applies when using Flux LoRA model)

enabled.description: Generate video motion from images instead of using static images

model.description: Which video model to use for motion generation

enabled.description: Enable AI voiceover for the video

captionsEnabled.description: Show text captions in the video

floating.description: Make the images move around slightly with floating effects

enabled.description: Add background music to the video

musicSlug.description: Choose background music for your video

loop.description: Whether to loop the background music

voice.description: Legacy: Voice ID for narration (use voiceoverConfig.voiceId instead)

captionsShow.description: Legacy: Whether to show captions (use captionsConfig.captionsEnabled instead)

captionsStyle.description: Legacy: Style for captions (use captionsConfig.captionsStyle instead)

effects.description: Legacy: Video effects configuration (use effectsConfig instead)

quality.description: Legacy: Video quality (handled by videoConfig now)

motion.description: Legacy: Motion configuration (handled by videoConfig now)

duration.description: Video duration (format varies by model - '5s' for Veo2, '5' for Kling, etc.)

negative_prompt.description: Negative prompt to avoid certain elements

imageDataUrl.description: Base64 data URL of input image for image-to-video models. Aliases `image_data_url` and `image` are also accepted and normalized.

imageUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the input image (interchangeable with imageDataUrl). The service will prioritize whichever field you supply before falling back to library attachments.

imageAttachmentId.description: Library attachment ID for input image

videoUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the input video (extend/edit/upscale). Preferred field name for source videos.

videoDataUrl.description: Base64 data URL of the input video.

video.description: Alternate video field accepted by select providers.

videoAttachmentId.description: Library attachment ID for input video.

prompt_optimizer.description: Whether to optimize the prompt (MiniMax model)

pro_mode.description: Enable pro mode for Hunyuan Video

seed.description: Optional model/provider-specific seed that may improve reproducibility where supported. Identical results are not guaranteed.

showExplicitContent.description: Allow explicit content (inverse of safety checker)

taskId.description: Run ID from the original Midjourney video request.

index.description: Zero-based index of the Midjourney video to extend (0-3).

runId.description: Unique identifier for the video generation request

model.description: The model used for generation

cost.description: Final cost for the video generation

task_id.description: The unique identifier for the Midjourney generation task.

status.description: The current status of the Midjourney task.

task_id.description: The unique identifier for the Midjourney generation task.

imageUrl.description: The URL of the generated image (present on SUCCESS).

failReason.description: The reason for failure (present on FAILED).

model.description: The model to use for generation

prompt.description: The text prompt to generate an image from

size.description: Requested output size or model-specific resolution value. Use GET /api/v1/image-models?detailed=true and read supported_parameters.resolutions for the selected model's supported values.

response_format.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Use "b64_json" (default) to receive base64-encoded image bytes in data[i].b64_json, or "url" to receive a time-limited, signed download URL in data[i].url (expires after a short period, currently ~1 hour). Note: When requesting "url", the API may still return "b64_json" if URL generation (upload/presign) fails, as a fallback.

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user

imageDataUrl.description: Base64-encoded image data URL for img2img generation. Single image input for models that support image-to-image transformation. Format: data:image/[type];base64,[data]. Note: Direct URL input is not supported - images must be converted to base64 data URLs before submission.

imageDataUrls.description: Array of base64-encoded image data URLs for models supporting multiple image inputs (e.g., flux-kontext, gpt-4o-image, gpt-image-1). Each URL must follow the format: data:image/[type];base64,[data]

maskDataUrl.description: Base64-encoded mask image data URL for inpainting models (e.g., flux-lora/inpainting). White areas indicate regions to edit. Format: data:image/[type];base64,[data]

strength.description: Controls how much the output differs from the input image in img2img mode. Lower values produce outputs closer to the input.

guidance_scale.description: How closely the model follows the text prompt. Higher values result in images more closely aligned with the prompt.

num_inference_steps.description: Number of denoising steps. More steps generally produce higher quality but take longer.

seed.description: Optional model-specific seed that may improve reproducibility where supported. Identical results are not guaranteed. Check the selected model's supported_parameters metadata before using this field.

kontext_max_mode.description: Enable enhanced context mode for flux-kontext model. Provides better understanding of input images.

created.description: Unix timestamp of when the image was created

data.description: List of generated images. Each entry contains either a hosted URL (data[i].url) or base64-encoded bytes (data[i].b64_json), never both.

url.description: Time-limited, signed download URL (presigned URL) to the generated image. It will expire after a short period (currently ~1 hour). If you need long-term access, download the image and store it yourself.

requestId.description: The unique request ID for the video generation

status.description: Current normalized status of the video generation

progress.description: Generation progress as a percentage (0-100), if available

estimatedTimeRemaining.description: Estimated time remaining in seconds, if available

videoUrl.description: URL to the generated video (available when status is 'completed')

error.description: Error message if the generation failed

createdAt.description: ISO timestamp when the request was created

completedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the generation completed

audio.description: Direct file upload (max 3MB). Supported formats: MP3, WAV, M4A, OGG, AAC

audioUrl.description: URL to audio file (alternative to direct upload, supports up to 500MB)

model.description: The STT model to use for transcription

language.description: Language code for transcription (ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-3). Use 'auto' for auto-detection

actualDuration.description: Actual audio duration in minutes for accurate billing

tagAudioEvents.description: Tag non-speech audio events like [laughter], [applause] (Elevenlabs-STT only)

audioUrl.description: URL to audio file to transcribe

X402AcceptOption.description: Legacy lower-level payment option offered in an x402 402 response.

X402PaymentAcceptedOption.description: Normalized public accountless x402 payment option.

invoice.description: Lightning invoice for lightning-l402 quotes. Same value as payTo for most clients.

paymentHash.description: Lightning payment hash for lightning-l402 quotes.

l402Token.description: L402 token to pair with the Lightning payment preimage in Authorization: L402 <token>:<preimage>.

discountRate.description: Discount rate applied to this payment option when present.

undiscountedAmountUsd.description: Undiscounted USD quote amount when a discount is applied.

feePayer.description: Solana facilitator fee payer for x402-solana-usdc quotes. Copy from the quote; do not hardcode.

X402StatusResponse.description: X-402 status response for polling-based payment flows.

audioUrl.description: URL to the generated audio file

contentType.description: MIME type of the audio file

model.description: Model used for generation

text.description: The input text that was synthesized

duration.description: Duration of the generated audio in seconds

urls.description: Array of YouTube URLs to transcribe (maximum 10 URLs per request)

transcripts.description: Array of transcript results for each URL

success.description: Whether the transcript was successfully retrieved

transcript.description: The full transcript text (only if successful)

requested.description: Number of URLs provided in the request

processed.description: Number of valid YouTube URLs found and processed

totalCost.description: Total cost in USD for successful transcripts

urls.description: Array of URLs to scrape (maximum 5 URLs per request)

results.description: Array of scraping results for each URL

markdown.description: Formatted markdown version of the content

requested.description: Number of URLs in the original request

processed.description: Number of valid URLs that were processed

failed.description: Number of URLs that failed to scrape

totalCost.description: Total cost in USD (only for successful scrapes)

provider.description: Search provider. Defaults to 'linkup'. 'openai-native' is not supported on /api/web.

depth.description: Search depth. For Linkup: standard ($0.006) or deep ($0.06). Linkup standard is executed as fast under the hood.

outputType.description: Output format. Linkup supports searchResults, sourcedAnswer, and structured. Non-Linkup providers currently support searchResults only.

structuredOutputSchema.description: JSON schema string defining the desired response format. Required when outputType is 'structured'

includeImages.description: Whether to include image results in the search

fromDate.description: Filter results from this date (YYYY-MM-DD format)

toDate.description: Filter results until this date (YYYY-MM-DD format)

excludeDomains.description: Array of domains to exclude from search results

includeDomains.description: Array of domains to search exclusively

oneOf.description: Array of search results when outputType is 'searchResults'

answer.description: The comprehensive answer to the query

oneOf.description: Structured data matching the provided schema

query.description: The search query that was executed

provider.description: The provider that executed the search

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the search was performed

cost.description: The cost of the search in USD

amount.description: Amount to deposit. Interpretation depends on ticker/provider (for example, crypto units for invoice-based deposits, or a USD/USDC amount for checkout-style deposits). Must be within the limits for the ticker.

paymentId.description: Encrypted Daimo payment identifier used by the client to launch the Daimo Pay widget

paymentLink.description: Stripe Checkout URL the user should open to complete payment

TransactionStatusResponse.description: Transaction status response. Fields vary by ticker/provider.

usdTo.description: Map of currency code to conversion rate (1 USD = rate * currency)

currencies.description: Map of currency code to display name

fiatEquivalentMinimum.description: Minimum deposit amount in USD equivalent

fiatEquivalentMaximum.description: Maximum deposit amount in USD equivalent

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of when limits were retrieved

oneOf.description: Array of text strings to embed (max 2048)

input.description: Text to embed - single string or array of up to 2048 strings

model.description: ID of the embedding model to use

dimensions.description: Reduce embedding dimensions (only for supported models)

index.description: Index of this embedding in the batch

oneOf.description: Embedding vector as array of floats

model.description: Model used for embeddings

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the input

description.example: Most cost-effective OpenAI embedding model with dimension reduction support

context_length.description: Maximum input tokens supported by the model

pricing.description: Pricing information for the moderation model

prompt.description: Cost per million input tokens in USD

completion.description: Cost per million output tokens in USD, when billable classification output applies

model.description: Moderation model ID. Omit to use the default moderation model.

oneOf.description: Array of text strings or content parts to classify. Batch support and supported modalities depend on the selected model.

model.description: Moderation model used for classification

results.description: Moderation results for each classified input

flagged.description: Whether the selected model classified the content as unsafe

category_scores.description: Model-dependent category-level confidence scores when available

usage.description: Token usage used for billing and diagnostics

completion_tokens.description: Output tokens billed or recorded, when applicable

message.example: Invalid request parameters. Please check your input and try again.

param.description: Request parameter related to the error, when available

expiration_days.description: Retention in days (1..365). Defaults to 30 if not provided.

model_context_limit.description: Target context size for compression (minimum enforced 10,000)

ProviderRoutingObject.description: Structured per-request provider routing controls. Per-request controls take precedence over saved provider preferences and are not merged with them.

order.description: Soft provider preference order. NanoGPT tries these providers first when possible, but this does not hard-pin routing to only these providers. Unknown providers are ignored.

only.description: Hard provider pin. NanoGPT restricts routing to these providers and fails instead of silently routing elsewhere. Unknown providers return a structured 400 error with code provider_unknown_provider.

ignore.description: Providers to exclude from routing. Unknown providers are ignored.

sort.description: Routing preference. speed, throughput, latency, and price actively request that strategy. auto, none, and default are accepted to suppress stored/default routing preferences without requesting a provider sort.

quantizations.description: Hard allowlist of model weight quantization levels. Providers without matching known quantization metadata are excluded unless unknown is included explicitly.

min_quantization.description: Minimum model weight quantization precision floor. Quantizations are ordered by bit width; integer and floating-point formats with the same bit width are treated as equivalent. unknown is not valid because it cannot be compared to a precision floor.

max_price.description: Optional provider price caps in USD per 1 million tokens. Pricing checks are best-effort when provider pricing is unknown.

prompt.description: Maximum prompt/input price in USD per 1 million tokens.

completion.description: Maximum completion/output price in USD per 1 million tokens.

allow_fallbacks.description: When false, disables cross-provider fallback for this request.

require_parameters.description: When true, requires the selected provider to support the requested parameters on routes that support parameter-level capability checks.

inputPer1kTokens.description: Price per 1K input tokens in USD

outputPer1kTokens.description: Price per 1K output tokens in USD

available.description: Whether the provider is currently available

supportsProviderSelection.description: Whether the model supports provider selection

excludedProviders.description: Providers that should never be used

enableFallback.description: Whether to fall back to the platform default if no preferred provider is available

excludedProviders.description: Providers that should never be used

enableFallback.description: Whether to fall back to the platform default if no preferred provider is available

modelOverrides.description: Per-model overrides for provider selection preferences

availableProviders.description: Provider IDs available to the current user for the model

excludedProviders.description: Providers that should never be used

enableFallback.description: Whether to fall back to the platform default if no preferred provider is available

modelOverrides.description: Per-model overrides for provider selection preferences

model.description: Model ID to use for the response. Provider-selection-capable models may include routing preference suffixes such as ':fast', ':speed', ':cheap', ':price', ':latency', ':throughput', ':floor', ':tools', ':caching', ':cache', or ':cached'.

billing_mode.description: Billing override to force pay-as-you-go without an explicit provider, or to apply saved provider preferences to subscription-included traffic. Accepted values (case-insensitive): paygo, pay-as-you-go, pay_as_you_go, paid, payg.

input.description: Prompt string or array of input items

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (not supported by reasoning models)

tools.description: Function tools available to the model

tool_choice.description: How the model should use tools

parallel_tool_calls.description: Allow multiple tool calls in parallel

store.description: Store the response locally for later retrieval/threading/background processing. Set false to disable stored Responses API data for the request.

retention_days.description: Per-request retention override in days (0..365). Use 0 to disable retention for the request; use null to fall back to configured defaults.

retentionDays.description: Alias for retention_days (0..365). If both are provided, values must match.

previous_response_id.description: Link to previous response for conversation threading

reasoning.description: Reasoning configuration. Setting `reasoning.effort` to any non-`none` value explicitly requests reasoning mode.

seed.description: Optional integer forwarded to providers that support seeded sampling. This may improve reproducibility but does not guarantee identical output. Results can change if NanoGPT selects a different route, uses a fallback, or the provider changes its backend.

service_tier.description: Optional service tier: "auto", "default", "flex", or "priority". Use "flex" for lower-cost variable-capacity processing or "priority" for higher-cost priority processing where supported by the routed model/provider.

ResponsesInfoResponse.description: Endpoint information for the Responses API

ResponsesResponse.description: Response object returned by the Responses API

advisor.description: Advisor orchestration status and per-phase usage. Present on responses to requests that use the Advisor extension.

amount.description: Credit amount to include with the invitation. Must be non-negative. Ignored for referral links (always 0).

currency.description: Currency for the amount. Required if amount > 0.

issuerName.description: Name of the person sending the invitation.

issuerNote.description: Personal note to include with the invitation.

insertId.description: Database ID of the created invitation.

redeemCode.description: Unique code the recipient uses to redeem the invitation.

url.description: Full URL to share with the recipient.

amount.description: Credit amount attached to this invitation.

currency.description: Currency of the amount, or null if amount is 0.
